speaker: SERGEANT
text: Sirs, take your places and be vigilant.

speaker: SERGEANT
text: If any noise or soldier you perceive

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Near to the walls, by some apparent sign

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Let us have knowledge at the court of guard.

speaker: SENTINEL
text: Sergeant, you shall.

speaker: SENTINEL
text: Thus are poor servitors,

speaker: SENTINEL
text: When others sleep upon their quiet beds,

speaker: SENTINEL
text: Constrained to watch in darkness, rain, and cold.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Lord Regent, and redoubted Burgundy,

speaker: TALBOT
text: By whose approach the regions of Artois,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Walloon, and Picardy are friends to us,

speaker: TALBOT
text: This happy night the Frenchmen are secure,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Having all day caroused and banqueted.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Embrace we then this opportunity,

speaker: TALBOT
text: As fitting best to quittance their deceit

speaker: TALBOT
text: Contrived by art and baleful sorcery.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Coward of France, how much he wrongs his fame,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Despairing of his own arm’s fortitude,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: To join with witches and the help of hell!

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Traitors have never other company.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: But what’s that Pucelle whom they term so pure?

speaker: TALBOT
text: A maid, they say.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: A maid? And be so martial?

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Pray God she prove not masculine ere long,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: If underneath the standard of the French

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: She carry armor as she hath begun.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Well, let them practice and converse with spirits.

speaker: TALBOT
text: God is our fortress, in whose conquering name

speaker: TALBOT
text: Let us resolve to scale their flinty bulwarks.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Ascend, brave Talbot. We will follow thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Not all together. Better far, I guess,

speaker: TALBOT
text: That we do make our entrance several ways,

speaker: TALBOT
text: That if it chance the one of us do fail,

speaker: TALBOT
text: The other yet may rise against their force.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Agreed. I’ll to yond corner.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: And I to this.

speaker: TALBOT
text: And here will Talbot mount, or make his grave.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now, Salisbury, for thee and for the right

speaker: TALBOT
text: Of English Henry, shall this night appear

speaker: TALBOT
text: How much in duty I am bound to both.

speaker: SENTINEL
text: Arm, arm! The enemy doth make assault.

speaker: ALANSON
text: How now, my lords? What, all unready so?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Unready? Ay, and glad we scaped so well.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: ’Twas time, I trow, to wake and leave our beds,

speaker: REIGNIER
text: Hearing alarums at our chamber doors.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Of all exploits since first I followed arms

speaker: ALANSON
text: Ne’er heard I of a warlike enterprise

speaker: ALANSON
text: More venturous or desperate than this.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I think this Talbot be a fiend of hell.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: If not of hell, the heavens sure favor him.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Here cometh Charles. I marvel how he sped.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Tut, holy Joan was his defensive guard.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Is this thy cunning, thou deceitful dame?

speaker: CHARLES
text: Didst thou at first, to flatter us withal,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Make us partakers of a little gain

speaker: CHARLES
text: That now our loss might be ten times so much?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Wherefore is Charles impatient with his friend?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: At all times will you have my power alike?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Sleeping or waking, must I still prevail,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Or will you blame and lay the fault on me?—

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Improvident soldiers, had your watch been good,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: This sudden mischief never could have fall’n.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Duke of Alanson, this was your default,

speaker: CHARLES
text: That, being captain of the watch tonight,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Did look no better to that weighty charge.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Had all your quarters been as safely kept

speaker: ALANSON
text: As that whereof I had the government,

speaker: ALANSON
text: We had not been thus shamefully surprised.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Mine was secure.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: And so was mine, my lord.

speaker: CHARLES
text: And for myself, most part of all this night

speaker: CHARLES
text: Within her quarter and mine own precinct

speaker: CHARLES
text: I was employed in passing to and fro

speaker: CHARLES
text: About relieving of the sentinels.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Then how or which way should they first break in?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Question, my lords, no further of the case,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: How or which way; ’tis sure they found some place

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But weakly guarded, where the breach was made.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And now there rests no other shift but this:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To gather our soldiers, scattered and dispersed,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: And lay new platforms to endamage them.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: I’ll be so bold to take what they have left.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: The cry of “Talbot” serves me for a sword,

speaker: SOLDIER
text: For I have loaden me with many spoils,

speaker: SOLDIER
text: Using no other weapon but his name.
